title: Simple verb aspect
description: Choose the simple aspect of the verb!
topic_path: Verb aspect: simple, progressive, and perfect

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the  [[☃ definition 1]] form of the missing verb.**

>Walter   \_\_\_\_\_\_ the purchase of a new car.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verb aspect** allows you to be more specific about when an action occurs. For example, "I was running" and "I ran" are both in the past tense, but "I was running" shows that the action was *ongoing* in the past.

**Simple aspect** verbs, whether in the past, present, or future tense, are just normally conjugated verbs. They don't use forms of "to have" or "to be" as helping verbs.
The simple form of the verb is **"ponders"**.

options.definition: Simple verbs are just normally conjugated verbs. They don't use helping verbs like "have" or "are".
